items.title: Is Microsoft a Deep Value Stock?
items.summary: Microsoft stock has declined nearly 20% year-to-date while tech peers rally, but the company's strong fundamentals remain intact. With AI business revenue at a $37 billion annual run rate (123% YoY growth) and a P/E ratio of 23.3—the second-lowest among Magnificent Seven stocks—Microsoft presents a potential deep value opportunity as cloud computing grows at a projected 16% CAGR through 2033.

items.title: Meta Platforms Is Quietly Becoming One of the Market's Best AI Stocks to Buy. Here Are 3 Reasons Why.
items.summary: Meta's AI capabilities are significantly boosting its core advertising business, with Q1 revenue rising 33% year-over-year driven by AI-improved content ranking and ad optimization. The company can rapidly deploy AI features to 3.5 billion daily users, and its massive $125-145 billion capital expenditure for AI infrastructure is funded by strong profitability. Trading at 22x earnings, Meta appears attractively valued despite risks from delayed developer APIs and nascent monetization of AI agents.

items.title: Bargain Alert: This Tech Stock's Bold AI Strategy Could Unlock Explosive Upside
items.summary: Meta Platforms is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and developing its own MTIA chip program. While current AI spending appears substantial, the custom chip strategy could provide long-term efficiency gains and cost advantages across ranking, recommendations, advertising, and generative AI workloads, potentially transforming the margin outlook.

items.title: Altrafin AG Acquires Shares of 6,002 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META - MarketBeat
items.summary: Altrafin AG Acquires Shares of 6,002 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META    MarketBeat

items.title: Dan Loeb Calls Selling Palantir In The $20s A 'Huge Mistake:' 'I Missed A 10X'
items.summary: Third Point CEO Dan Loeb admitted to prematurely selling Palantir Technologies shares in the $20 range before its IPO, missing out on a 10x return as the stock rallied significantly post-public listing. Loeb also reflected on broader challenges in managing high-growth tech investments and the constraints of public market liquidity, citing his firm's experience with Upstart as a cautionary tale about board restrictions limiting trading flexibility.

items.title: Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Is A Top AI Stock In Ken Griffin’s Portfolio - Insider Monkey
items.summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Is A Top AI Stock In Ken Griffin’s Portfolio    Insider Monkey

items.title: History Offers a $974 Billion Warning Ahead of the Game-Changing SpaceX IPO
items.summary: SpaceX is set to go public on June 12, 2026, at $135 per share with a $1.77 trillion valuation. While structural factors like fast-tracked index inclusion and low float could boost the stock initially, historical data shows that 30 major tech IPOs over 14 years experienced an average 55% drawdown in their first year, suggesting investors should be cautious about chasing the IPO hype.

items.title: Is Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) A Good Stock To Buy Now? - Insider Monkey
items.summary: Is Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) A Good Stock To Buy Now?    Insider Monkey

items.title: 2 Glorious Growth Stocks to Buy During the Latest Tech Sell-Off
items.summary: During a tech market downturn with the Nasdaq Composite down 7.5% from its peak, Meta Platforms and Lemonade are presented as attractive investment opportunities. Meta is leveraging AI to boost user engagement and advertising revenue, trading at a P/E ratio of 21.3 compared to the Nasdaq-100's 35.3. Lemonade is using AI to transform insurance operations, achieving record growth with 3.1 million policyholders and accelerating in-force premium growth for 10 consecutive quarters.

items.title: Meta Stock: New Subscriptions Could Boost Revenue Growth
items.summary: Meta Platforms has unveiled new subscription plans across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp to address investor concerns about monetization and revenue growth. Consumer Plus tiers range from $2.99-$3.99/month, while Meta One plans ($7.99-$19.99/month) offer enhanced AI features. Business and creator tiers ($14.99-$49.99/month) are being tested in select markets. Even modest adoption rates could meaningfully impact Meta's $215 billion annual revenue, though the stock remains down 10% year-to-date amid uncertainty over AI spending.

items.title: 3 High-Growth Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy With $5,000 Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends three AI-related stocks for a $5,000 investment: Micron and SanDisk, which benefit from memory chip shortages driven by AI buildout with projected revenue growth of 264-337% over the next two quarters, and CoreWeave, a data center operator renting GPU computing capacity with $100 billion in contracted revenue over 5-6 years and estimated growth of 112-154% in the next two quarters.

items.title: 3 Reasons to Buy SpaceX Stock at Its IPO -- and 2 Reasons to Wait
items.summary: SpaceX is set to go public on June 12, 2026, with a diverse portfolio including space launch services, social media platform X, AI platform Grok, and Starlink satellite internet. While the company generates positive operating cash flow and operates multiple promising businesses, investors should be cautious about typical post-IPO volatility and the difficulty in assessing a rapidly evolving company with potential future changes like a possible Tesla merger.

items.title: If You Invested $10,000 in Meta Platforms Stock 10 Years Ago, Here's How Much You'd Have Today
items.summary: Meta Platforms has delivered a 434% total return over the past decade, turning a $10,000 investment in June 2016 into $53,380 today, outpacing the S&P 500. Despite current volatility with shares down 8% in 2026 and 23% from their August peak, the company's 3.56 billion monthly active users and attractive P/E ratio of 22.8 present a buying opportunity, though investors remain concerned about the company's massive $125-145 billion capital expenditure plans for AI infrastructure expansion.

items.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities Co. Ltd. Acquires Shares of 876 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META - MarketBeat
items.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities Co. Ltd. Acquires Shares of 876 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META    MarketBeat

items.summary: 1,365 Shares in Meta Platforms, Inc. $META Acquired by TrustBank    MarketBeat

items.title: Meta Stock Is Getting Hit Hard. But Where Will It Be in 3 Years?
items.summary: Meta's stock has fallen 25% from its all-time high despite strong business fundamentals, with Q1 advertising revenue growing 33% year-over-year. However, massive capital expenditure plans ($125-145 billion in 2026, nearly double 2025 spending) and Reality Labs losses ($19.2 billion in 2025) are pressuring profits and investor sentiment. The stock could trade anywhere from mid-$500s to $1,000 over the next three years depending on whether AI investments translate into profitable new products.

items.title: 3 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next Decade
items.summary: The article recommends Meta Platforms, Microsoft, and Broadcom as solid long-term AI investments. Meta's ad revenue accelerated to 33% growth driven by AI improvements, Microsoft's AI business reached a $37 billion annual run rate with strong Azure growth, and Broadcom's AI semiconductor revenue is expected to exceed $100 billion by fiscal 2027. All three companies generate sufficient cash flow to sustain heavy AI investments over the next decade.

items.title: 3 Top-Tech Stocks to Buy With $1,000 Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Nvidia as compelling investment opportunities for investors with $1,000 to deploy. Microsoft's AI business is growing at 123% with a $37 billion annual run rate but trades 15% below all-time highs at attractive valuations. Meta Platforms is undervalued relative to its strong ad business growth (33% revenue growth) and potential AI investments. Nvidia, despite being the world's largest company, shows accelerating revenue growth at 85% and is positioned to benefit from massive projected increases in data center capital expenditures through 2030.

items.title: Meta Platforms Is Down 21% From Its All-Time High: Here's What History Says Will Happen Next
items.summary: Meta Platforms stock has declined 21% from its all-time high amid investor concerns about significant capex spending and its return on investment. However, historical analysis shows that Meta has consistently bounced back and outperformed the S&P 500 after previous major declines (32% in 2018, 30% in 2020, and 75% in 2021-2022). The company's massive user base, strong competitive advantages, and ability to pivot strategies—such as cutting costs during the metaverse disappointment and pivoting to AI—suggest it could deliver strong returns for investors buying at current levels.

items.title: Broadcom Just Crashed 15%. Here's What It Means for Every AI Investor
items.summary: Broadcom stock plummeted 15% following its Q2 2026 earnings report, despite AI revenue surging 143%. The sell-off also impacted other AI and cybersecurity stocks including CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks. The article examines why this major decline occurred and what it signals for AI investors going forward.

items.title: SpaceX IPO: Trade the Blast Radius
items.summary: SpaceX is set to go public on June 12 with an estimated $2 trillion valuation and $75 billion raise. While many prop traders won't have direct access to the IPO, the article suggests trading the broader market effects rather than the stock itself. The IPO could boost Nasdaq sentiment through forced buying when SpaceX is added to the Nasdaq-100 index, but historical precedent shows mega-IPOs often experience significant drawdowns after initial hype fades. Traders are advised to focus on Nasdaq futures, QQQ, and related technology stocks rather than chasing the IPO directly.

items.title: Insider Sell: Robert Kimmitt Sells 504 Shares of Meta Platforms Inc (META) - GuruFocus
items.summary: Insider Sell: Robert Kimmitt Sells 504 Shares of Meta Platforms Inc (META)    GuruFocus

items.title: How Index "Gaming" Around S&P Additions Could Hurt Passive Investors -- and Smart Ways to Defend Your Portfolio
items.summary: Index reconstitutions can distort stock prices when new companies are added to the S&P 500 at stretched valuations, creating risks for passive investors. The article suggests defensive strategies including equal-weight ETFs, stricter indexes, and customized accounts to mitigate these pressures.

items.title: Broadcom's AI Revenue Doubled — And It May Not Even Be Halfway Done
items.summary: Broadcom reported $8.4 billion in AI semiconductor revenue in Q1 FY2026, up 106% year-over-year, with Q2 guidance of $10.7 billion. The company has a $73 billion AI backlog and custom silicon partnerships with major hyperscalers including Meta, OpenAI, Anthropic, and ByteDance. Citi projects AI revenue could reach $115 billion in fiscal 2027 and $180 billion in fiscal 2028, representing 81% of total sales.

items.title: Remitly's Path to Profitability Is Becoming Clearer. Here's What's Driving It.
items.summary: Remitly, a digital cross-border remittance provider, has achieved profitability in 2025 after years of losses, driven by user growth from 2.8M to 9.3M customers, economies of scale, AI-powered fraud detection, and ecosystem expansion through WhatsApp integration and new services. The company is expected to grow revenue at 19% CAGR through 2028 with adjusted EBITDA and GAAP EPS growing at 30% and 54% CAGRs respectively, though it faces competition from traditional money transfer services and emerging stablecoins.

items.title: Fideuram Intesa Sanpaolo Private Banking S.P.A. Acquires Shares of 34,056 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META - MarketBeat
items.summary: Fideuram Intesa Sanpaolo Private Banking S.P.A. Acquires Shares of 34,056 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META    MarketBeat

items.title: 1 Nuclear Energy Stock to Buy and Hold for the Next 20 Years
items.summary: Oklo, a small modular nuclear reactor developer, is positioned to capitalize on growing data center power demands. The company has partnered with Meta to develop up to 1.2 gigawatts of nuclear capacity, with first reactors potentially online by 2030. While regulatory approval and demonstration of its Aurora reactor technology remain pending, the nuclear power market could represent a multitrillion-dollar opportunity as data centers require massive amounts of clean, reliable energy.

items.title: Softbank's Masayoshi Son Isn't Worried About An AI Bubble— He's Hunting For The Next Trillion-Dollar Industry: 'Just The Beginning'
items.summary: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son believes the AI revolution is '50x bigger' than the dot-com boom and will last 50-100 years, dismissing concerns of an AI bubble. Son has identified humanoid and industrial robotics with 'physical AI' as the next trillion-dollar opportunity, evidenced by SoftBank's $5.4 billion acquisition of ABB's robotics division in October and plans for a $100 billion IPO of its Roze AI venture.

items.title: Oracle Joins Arm's AGI CPU Ecosystem As Agentic AI Boom Draws Meta, OpenAI And ByteDance
items.summary: Oracle has joined Arm Holdings' AGI CPU ecosystem, with CEO Rene Haas announcing that ByteDance is also now a customer. Arm's AGI CPUs, which offer more than twice the performance per rack of traditional x86 CPUs, are gaining significant traction among major tech companies including Meta, OpenAI, and Cerebras. The momentum is driven by the shift toward agentic AI systems, with Arm's energy-efficient architecture positioned to benefit from this industry evolution.

items.title: Meta’s 20% De-Rating Looks Hard to Defend Against 33% Revenue Growth
items.summary: Meta stock has declined 20% from its August 2025 peak despite posting exceptional Q1 results with 33% revenue growth and 57% earnings beat. The selloff was driven by elevated capex guidance ($135-145B for 2026) rather than fundamental weakness. The stock is testing critical resistance at $650, with a bullish falling-wedge pattern suggesting potential recovery. Trading at 22.99x trailing P/E and 17-18x forward 2027 earnings, Meta appears undervalued given its dominant advertising business, record engagement across 4B users, and AI-driven pricing power.

items.title: Why Oklo Is an Asymmetric AI Bet With a 'Nuclear Option'
items.summary: Oklo Inc., a pre-revenue nuclear power company valued at $12 billion, is developing small modular reactors to power AI data centers. The company has secured non-binding agreements with Meta and Switch but faces significant regulatory hurdles, execution risks, and won't generate revenue until at least 2028. Success depends on NRC licensing approval and avoiding construction delays common in nuclear projects.

items.title: Wilian Securities LLC Acquires New Holdings in Meta Platforms, Inc. $META - MarketBeat
items.summary: Wilian Securities LLC Acquires New Holdings in Meta Platforms, Inc. $META    MarketBeat

items.title: Is Oklo Stock Your Ticket to Becoming a Millionaire?
items.summary: Oklo, a nuclear microreactor startup, has experienced significant volatility after rising over 700% year-to-date before declining from its October peak. While the company has secured partnerships with Meta, Switch, and Equinix and boasts a 14-gigawatt customer pipeline, it lacks regulatory approval and revenue. With commercial operations potentially two years away and uncertain economics, investors face substantial risk despite the company's innovative business model of deploying small reactors for on-site power generation.

items.title: Consumer Tech News (May 25-29): Dell, IBM, CrowdStrike, Meta Lead Big Tech AI Push
items.summary: Major tech companies drove AI innovation this week with strong earnings and strategic initiatives. Dell reported record Q1 revenue of $43.84B, significantly beating estimates, while IBM and Red Hat launched a $5B open-source security initiative. Meta expanded into paid subscriptions across its platforms, and CrowdStrike expanded its cybersecurity initiatives. Anthropic surpassed OpenAI as the world's most valuable startup at $965B valuation. Japanese banks gained access to OpenAI's GPT-5.5 model for cybersecurity defense.

items.title: 1 AI Stock That Could Turn $500 per Month Into $1 Million
items.summary: Nebius Group is positioned as a potential long-term AI infrastructure winner with 684% YoY revenue growth and multibillion-dollar commitments from Meta ($27B) and Microsoft ($17.4B). While achieving the $1M return target would require 18.4% annualized returns over 20 years, Nebius' strong demand visibility and Nvidia backing support its growth prospects, though significant capital spending ($20-25B in 2026) poses execution and dilution risks.

items.title: AMD’s AI Momentum Is Testing the Limits of Its Valuation
items.summary: AMD stock surged 6% to near all-time highs, driven by strong AI infrastructure demand and data-center growth projections showing 44% current-year revenue growth accelerating to 53% next year. However, the stock trades at overbought technical levels (RSI 72.7) and faces emerging competitive threats from Nvidia's planned entry into the standalone server-CPU market, which could pressure AMD's most profitable EPYC franchise. While AMD's AI accelerators and strategic partnerships validate its position as Nvidia's primary challenger, valuation concerns and competitive pressures create tension between momentum and fundamentals.

items.title: 2 Top Tech Stocks to Buy Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) and Meta Platforms as two compelling tech stock picks. TSMC benefits from its near-monopoly in advanced AI chip manufacturing with strong profit growth, while Meta trades at an undervalued 18.9x forward earnings despite a solid core advertising business growing 33% year-over-year, though investor concerns about its elevated capex spending persist.

items.title: Trump Ally Laura Loomer Questions Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's Chinese University Ties, Writes To Pentagon, Says It's A 'Massive Scandal'
items.summary: Trump ally Laura Loomer has raised concerns about Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's access to the White House through the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST) while simultaneously joining the advisory board of Tsinghua University, a Chinese university with embedded Communist Party governance. Loomer claims to have reported this to the Pentagon, calling it a 'massive scandal.' Huang has previously acknowledged that Nvidia has largely conceded the Chinese AI chip market due to export controls.

items.title: AMD: Strong AI Sales, But the Win Is Already Priced In
items.summary: AMD delivered strong Q1 2026 results with 38% YoY revenue growth and record free cash flow, driven by booming Data Center segment revenues up 57% YoY. However, the stock at $430 trades at a 78x forward P/E and 35x future free cash flow multiple, requiring simultaneous execution on multiple fronts: sustained 35%+ Data Center growth, 55%+ gross margins, successful MI450 GPU ramp, and no further China headwinds. While the company's transformation is real, much of the progress is already priced into the valuation.

items.title: Is Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Entering a New AI Growth Phase After Workforce Restructuring? - Insider Monkey
items.summary: Is Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Entering a New AI Growth Phase After Workforce Restructuring?    Insider Monkey

items.title: Meta Rallies as Subscription Plans Put AI Profitability in Focus
items.summary: Meta stock rallies 3.74% following announcements of new subscription plans for Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Meta AI. The subscription model addresses investor concerns about whether the company's massive AI capital expenditure ($125-145B in 2026) can translate into profitability. Meta's Q1 showed $56.31B revenue with strong ad metrics but high capex of $19.84B, creating pressure on free cash flow. The recurring revenue from subscriptions provides a new growth avenue beyond advertising.

items.title: Odyssey Capital Advisors Inc. Acquires Shares of 1,028 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META - MarketBeat
items.summary: Odyssey Capital Advisors Inc. Acquires Shares of 1,028 Meta Platforms, Inc. $META    MarketBeat

items.title: 4 Brilliant Artificial Intelligence Stocks That 1 Billionaire Just Bought
items.summary: Billionaire Chase Coleman's Tiger Global Management recently increased positions in four AI stocks: Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, Meta Platforms, and Broadcom. The article highlights why each company is well-positioned for AI growth, citing strong revenue growth, reasonable valuations, and significant future potential in AI infrastructure and custom chip development.

items.title: NuScale Power vs. Oklo: Which Nuclear Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: The article compares two nuclear energy stocks—NuScale Power and Oklo—both positioned to benefit from AI data center energy demand and government backing for nuclear expansion. While NuScale holds regulatory approval for its small modular reactor design, Oklo is recommended as the better buy due to stronger partnerships with the DOE and Nvidia, operational progress toward criticality, and a larger cash balance of $2 billion.

items.title: Meta Platforms May Be the Most Undervalued Big Tech Stock in the Market
items.summary: Meta Platforms is trading at a cheap valuation (12.6x operating cash flow) despite strong fundamentals. The company's legacy advertising business is thriving with 33% revenue growth driven by AI integration, while Reality Labs continues to lose billions. The author argues the market undervalues Meta and suggests potential catalysts like AI products or glasses could trigger significant stock appreciation.

items.title: Missed Out on the Initial AI Rally? These 3 AI Stocks Are Just Getting Started.
items.summary: Despite the strong AI sector rally since April, three stocks still offer significant upside potential: Meta Platforms trades at a discount to the S&P 500 with 33% revenue growth; Micron is capitalizing on memory chip shortages with expected 193% revenue growth this year; and Nebius is experiencing explosive growth with its AI-focused neocloud platform, projecting 550% growth this year.

items.title: Meta's $125 Billion AI Bet Might Be The Capex Trap Of The Decade
items.summary: Meta has doubled its capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion for 2026 to fund AI infrastructure, but investors remain skeptical about the company's ability to monetize these investments. While Meta's core advertising business shows strong fundamentals with 33% revenue growth and 82% gross margins, the massive spending on chips and data centers amid simultaneous workforce cuts raises concerns about ROI. The company faces a 'chipflation' crisis with DRAM prices skyrocketing 17-fold, forcing Meta to diversify its hardware suppliers across Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, and others.

items.title: After a Q1 Blowout and a Soaring Share Price, Is AMD Stock Still a Buy?
items.summary: AMD's stock has more than doubled year-to-date following strong Q1 earnings with 38% revenue growth and a 57% surge in data center revenue. While the company shows impressive fundamentals with record free cash flow and major customer wins from Meta and OpenAI, the stock's valuation at 150x trailing earnings and 59x forward earnings leaves little room for error. The article suggests AMD could still be a buy for believers in continued AI infrastructure spending, but recommends keeping new positions modest given the sharp rally and pullback risks.

items.title: CoreWeave vs. Nebius: Which Artificial Intelligence (AI) Infrastructure Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: Nebius Group is recommended as the better AI infrastructure investment in 2026 compared to CoreWeave. While CoreWeave has higher revenue ($5.1B) and scale, it carries significant risks including massive debt ($20B+), customer concentration (67% from Microsoft), and ongoing losses ($1.2B net loss). Nebius, though smaller ($529.8M revenue), demonstrates profitability ($101.7M net income), lower debt ($4B), and faster growth (479% YoY), making it a financially healthier choice despite trading at a premium valuation.

items.title: Is Microsoft or Meta Platforms the Greatest Value on the Market?
items.summary: Both Microsoft and Meta Platforms are trading below their normal valuation ranges, presenting potential buying opportunities. Microsoft's AI business is already generating significant revenue through Copilot and Azure cloud services, while Meta's AI upside potential is greater but not yet monetized. Using price-to-cash-flow metrics, Microsoft is identified as the better value despite Meta being cheaper, as Microsoft's AI business is proven and should trade at its historical upper-20s valuation multiple.

items.title: This AI Stock Is So Cheap Right Now It Feels Like a Glitch. Here's Why I'm Buying.
items.summary: Meta Platforms is trading at an attractive valuation of less than 13 times cash from operations despite strong fundamentals. The company's advertising business is growing rapidly with 33% revenue growth in Q1 driven by AI improvements. While the market criticizes Meta's high capital expenditure on AI infrastructure, the author believes upcoming products like superintelligence AI models and AI glasses could drive significant stock appreciation, making it a major buying opportunity.

items.title: SpaceX Is Kicking Off IPO Mania -- but It's Also Reviving Stock-Split Euphoria
items.summary: SpaceX is accelerating its IPO to June 12, 2026, aiming to raise $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation and become the eighth-largest U.S. public company. The company will conduct a 5-for-1 forward stock split to improve retail investor accessibility. However, the article warns that despite historical stock-split outperformance, mega-IPOs typically struggle post-debut, and SpaceX's expected astronomical price-to-sales ratio may signal a bubble.

items.title: Mistral AI CEO Warns Europe Has Two Years To Avoid Becoming US 'Vassal State:' Here's Why
items.summary: Mistral AI CEO Arthur Mensch warned during a French National Assembly hearing that Europe has only two years to build independent AI infrastructure before becoming dependent on U.S. tech giants. He highlighted that the battle for AI dominance is fundamentally about controlling chips, energy, and compute resources, with U.S. firms deploying approximately $1 trillion next year. Mensch also cited Europe's fragmented regulations and shallow capital markets as critical barriers to competing at scale.

items.title: SpaceX Accelerates Its IPO Timeline: 10 Things You Need to Know
items.summary: SpaceX is accelerating its IPO timeline with plans to debut on June 12, 2026, on the Nasdaq under ticker symbol SPCX. The company aims to raise up to $75 billion, making it 2.5 times larger than Saudi Aramco's previous record IPO. SpaceX approved a 5-for-1 stock split and will benefit from Nasdaq's new fast-entry rule allowing inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 after just 15 trading sessions. However, historical precedent suggests large IPOs often struggle in their first six months post-debut.

items.title: These 2 Nvidia-Backed Stocks Look Like Genius Investments
items.summary: CoreWeave and Nebius, two neocloud providers backed by Nvidia, are experiencing rapid growth in AI computing infrastructure. CoreWeave grew revenue 112% to $2.1B in Q1 with a $100B backlog, while Nebius achieved 684% revenue growth to $399M. Both companies face profitability challenges due to high capital expenditure requirements but show strong long-term potential as AI demand continues.

items.title: Arm Holdings CEO Rene Haas Has a Big Warning for Intel and AMD
items.summary: Arm Holdings is entering the server CPU market with its new AGI CPU, claiming it can reduce AI data center capital costs by up to $10 billion per gigawatt with over 2x performance per rack compared to x86 platforms. While Arm has secured major customers like Meta and expects $15 billion in annual revenue by 2031, AMD and Intel currently dominate with significantly larger data center revenues ($5.8B and $5.1B respectively in Q1). The market is large enough for multiple players, but Arm's energy-efficient architecture poses a competitive threat to the x86 incumbents.

items.title: Meta vs. Snap: What Do Their Quarterly Revenue Trends Tell Investors?
items.summary: Meta and Snap show contrasting revenue trajectories, with Meta reporting 33% year-over-year Q1 revenue growth to $56.3 billion versus Snap's modest 12% growth to $1.5 billion. Meta's profitability (48% net margin) and successful AI investments in hardware stand in stark contrast to Snap's unprofitability (-6% net margin), making Meta a more appealing investment despite both companies operating in the social media advertising space.

items.title: Better Datacenter Stock: CoreWeave (CRWV) or Nebius (NBIS)?
items.summary: CoreWeave and Nebius are both AI infrastructure companies experiencing rapid growth, but the article recommends Nebius as the better buy. CoreWeave has impressive revenue growth but faces widening losses and a high debt-to-equity ratio of 10.7. Nebius shows stronger growth prospects, lower debt (2.1 ratio), and is expected to achieve profitability by 2028, despite trading at a higher valuation multiple.

items.title: 1 Incredible Bargain Hiding Right Under Investors' Noses
items.summary: Meta Platforms is presented as an undervalued investment opportunity despite strong 30%+ revenue growth. The article argues that while the market is concerned about Meta's high capital expenditures on AI and Reality Labs, the company's AI improvements to its advertising business are already delivering results. With a forward P/E ratio of less than 19 compared to the S&P 500's 21.9, Meta trades cheaper than the broader market despite superior growth. The author suggests Meta's upcoming AI superintelligence and smart glasses products could unlock additional value not yet priced into the stock.

items.title: CoreWeave's Stock Is Plunging. Should You Buy the Dip?
items.summary: CoreWeave's stock fell over 10% in May after the company issued lighter-than-expected Q2 guidance and announced higher capital expenditures, despite revenue more than doubling year-over-year. While the company has a strong $100 billion backlog and partnerships with Meta, Nvidia, and Anthropic, concerns about rising losses, heavy debt, and margin pressure are weighing on investor sentiment. The article suggests high-risk investors may find value in the dip, while conservative investors should wait on the sidelines.

items.title: Which AI Supercycle Stock Will Make You Richer Over the Next 10 Years?
items.summary: AMD and Intel are both benefiting from the AI supercycle, but AMD emerges as the more dependable 10-year investment. AMD posted accelerating revenue growth of 38% YoY with data center revenue up 57%, strong customer commitments, and robust free cash flow. Intel shows turnaround signs with a 22% data center growth, but its foundry business is unprofitable and still needs to prove its manufacturing strategy can work at scale. AMD's growth is visible today while Intel's valuation already prices in future success.

items.title: Arm Just Made a Bold Prediction, And It Could Be a Huge Buy Signal
items.summary: Arm Holdings forecasts becoming the CPU market leader by decade's end, with CEO Rene Haas predicting it will have the largest market share by CPU type. The company's new AGI CPU has demand exceeding $2 billion, more than double initial expectations. While supply chain constraints limit near-term fulfillment, Arm projects $15 billion in CPU revenue by fiscal 2031 and $25 billion in total revenue, with potential to significantly exceed these conservative forecasts.

items.title: Deal Dispatch: J.M. Smucker Considers Strategic Review, Meta Buys Assured Robot Intelligence, Buzzfeed On Brink Of Bankruptcy
items.summary: Multiple companies announced strategic reviews and M&A activity. J.M. Smucker hired Goldman Sachs for a portfolio review amid activist pressure, potentially leading to a Hostess sale. Meta acquired Assured Robot Intelligence for AI robotics development. Meanwhile, BuzzFeed faces bankruptcy risk after missing a $5 million debt payment with a May 18 deadline. Other notable deals include GameStop's $55 billion non-binding proposal for eBay, Angelini Pharma's $4.1 billion acquisition of Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, and Ametek's $5 billion purchase of Indicor's instrumentation businesses.

items.title: 2 Outstanding AI Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next Decade
items.summary: The article recommends Microsoft and Meta Platforms as two AI stocks worth buying and holding for the next decade. Despite recent stock price declines and investor concerns about heavy AI spending, both companies are showing strong financial performance with robust revenue growth driven by AI initiatives. Microsoft's Azure posted 40% year-over-year growth and its AI business has a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, while Meta's revenue grew 33% year-over-year. Both companies are positioned to deliver excellent long-term returns despite near-term market skepticism.

items.title: Meta's Earnings Got a Major Tax Boost. Here Are the Adjusted Figures You Need to See.
items.summary: Meta reported strong Q1 2026 revenue growth of 33% year-over-year, but an $8.03 billion tax benefit inflated reported earnings. Excluding this benefit, adjusted EPS growth was only 14%, significantly trailing revenue growth. The company raised 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion (nearly double 2025 spending), driven by higher component costs and data center investments. While the ad business accelerates and valuation appears reasonable at 20.5x forward P/E, the massive spending increase presents significant risk.

items.title: Thinking of Buying Pinterest? Stop and Do This Instead.
items.summary: Despite beating Q1 earnings expectations, Pinterest's growth is underwhelming compared to industry peers like Meta. The company remains unprofitable on a GAAP basis with high stock-based compensation (23% of revenue), and faces headwinds from AI-generated content. Meta significantly outperformed with 33% revenue growth and superior profit margins, making it a better investment choice.

items.title: 3 Next Generation Nuclear Energy Stocks Investors Should Be Watching Closely
items.summary: As global energy demand surges, three nuclear energy companies are positioned to lead the next-generation reactor market. Oklo develops advanced microreactors with strong customer demand but faces multi-year delays before operations. NuScale holds regulatory approval as the only certified SMR developer but needs to convert partnerships into binding deals. GE Vernova, an established power company, is actively constructing its first commercial SMR in Canada with near-term revenue from gas turbines funding development.

items.title: Meta's Revenue Growth Is Impressive, but Its AI Budget Is Getting Harder to Ignore
items.summary: Meta reported impressive 33% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, but the stock declined following the announcement due to escalating AI infrastructure spending. The company raised its full-year 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion (nearly double 2025's $72 billion), citing higher component and data center costs. While management emphasizes efficiency improvements through custom silicon and AMD chips, investors remain concerned about whether these massive investments will generate adequate returns.

items.title: Why the iShares Semiconductor ETF Jumped 40% in April
items.summary: The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) surged 40% in April 2026, driven by strong semiconductor demand fueled by AI adoption. Key catalysts included impressive earnings reports from Intel, TSMC, and NXP, major cloud infrastructure deals for CoreWeave, and increased capital expenditure forecasts from hyperscalers like Meta. However, the sector now trades at a high valuation with a P/E ratio of 52.3.

items.title: Why Meta Platforms Stock Plummeted This Week
items.summary: Meta Platforms stock fell 9.8% this week despite beating Q1 earnings and revenue expectations. The decline was driven by concerns over a sequential decline in daily active users (attributed to disruptions in Iran and WhatsApp restrictions in Russia) and Meta's announcement of increased capital expenditure targets for 2026, raising capex guidance to $125-145 billion from $115-135 billion to fund AI investments.

items.title: What Could Happen if You Put $10,000 Into Oklo Today?
items.summary: Oklo, a nuclear technology company developing micro reactors for data centers, has a $13 billion market valuation with zero revenue and lacks regulatory approval. While the company has strong backing from Meta and a $14 GW customer backlog, investing $10,000 at current prices is highly risky—potential gains could be substantial but losses could be equally severe given the pre-commercialization stage and $100 million annual cash burn.
